data.title: Apple (AAPL) Stock Under FTC Scrutiny Over Apple News Bias Claims
data.description:
Apple Inc. (AAPL) is facing fresh attention from U.S. regulators over how its Apple News app selects and shows stories. This week, Federal Trade Commission (FTC) chair Andrew Ferguson sent a letter to Chief Executive Tim Cook, raising concerns that Apple may be treating news outlets in an unfair way.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In the letter, Ferguson pointed to reports claiming Apple News has promoted "leftist outlets" while leaving out more conservative sources. He warned that "any act or practice by Apple News to suppress or promote news articles based on the perceived ideological or political viewpoint" may break consumer laws.

Meanwhile, AAPL shares rose slightly on Wednesday, closing at $275.50.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Down But Not Out: Piper Sandler Names Its Top 2 Software Stocks for 2026
data.description:
The rapid expansion of AI in recent years has created a more complicated backdrop for the software industry. While AI continues to drive productivity gains and new revenue opportunities across technology, it is also introducing structural pressures.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

By improving efficiencies, AI systems are reducing headcounts – and that has implications for software companies, who sell subscriptions and products on a per-seat basis. The situation is also complicated on the coding side, where AI makes it possible for customers to code their own apps, rather than buying purpose-built software.

In a recent note on the software sector, Piper Sandler analyst Billy Fitzsimmons outlined the firm's current take on this beaten-down segment.

"Despite broad-based pullbacks after negative reactions to the initial C4Q prints, we still we believe select pockets of software are better insulated from AI disruption. AI demand is outpacing supply and AI investments are increasing compute/ storage requirements, benefitting the hyperscalers and consumption growth. Meanwhile, vertical software names possess specialized workflows, proprietary data, and strong customer relationships. The customers for specific verticals (the trades, construction) were later SaaS adopters, so it is hard to see them as early vibe coding trailblazers," the analyst opined.

With that framework in mind, Fitzsimmons highlights two software stocks he believes can buck the broader trend and post gains in 2026, calling both his top picks. One is a well-known name, while the other flies more under the radar – but both carry Buy ratings from Piper Sandler, and as a check of the TipRanks database shows – from Wall Street overall.

Microsoft (MSFT)

We'll start with a look at Microsoft, the world's largest software firm – and one of the largest tech companies in the world. Microsoft's $3.07 trillion market cap ranks it as the fourth-largest publicly traded firm on Wall Street. While Microsoft originally built itself up on the success of its software products – Windows and Office are still the gold standard in PC software – it's been finding strong success recently in the related fields of AI and cloud computing.

On the AI side, we should note that Microsoft was an early backer of the technology, and put its money where its mouth was. Microsoft started funding OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT, with a $1 billion investment in 2019. In the years since, the software giant has backed OpenAI to the tune of over $13 billion – and last year, the two companies formalized their relationship for the future. Microsoft now owns a 27% stake in the AI firm, and OpenAI has committed to spending $250 billion over the next several years on Microsoft's Azure cloud computing platform. The overall agreement, for Microsoft, represents a massive return on its original investment.

It also underscores the growing importance of Azure to Microsoft. The subscription-based cloud platform has become one of Microsoft's hottest products, and the company's Intelligent Cloud segment, which includes Azure, now accounts for just over 40% of total revenues.

Microsoft is responding to this growth by investing heavily in AI and cloud data centers, which brings us to a sore point for investors and analysts. In the past year, the company has implemented a 65% jump in capital expenditure on data centers, to a total of $37.5 billion. That's a massive outlay, by any standards, and while the company's goal of doubling its data center footprint is laudable, the sheer scale of the spending has prompted worries that it may not be sustainable.

Turning to the company's last quarterly report, which covered fiscal 2Q26, we find that the top line, of $81.3 billion, was up 17% year-over-year and beat expectations by $993.7 million. Microsoft realized non-GAAP earnings of $4.14 per share, for a 24% year-over-year gain – and 22 cents per share better than the forecast. Intelligent Cloud revenue was listed as $32.9 billion, up 29% year-over-year.

We should note that, despite the strong figures, Microsoft's stock has slipped by 16% since the earnings report. As noted, worries about the size and expense of the data center expansion along with Azure growth that slightly missed expectations lie behind that dip.

The recent softness in Microsoft's stock is a flashing neon sign for Piper Sandler, saying 'opportunity.' Analyst Fitzsimmons writes of the company, "We would remain buyers on any MSFT weakness given 1) AI demand continues to exceed supply (the Azure growth rate as largely handicapped by capacity not slowing demand) and 2) total capex growth is expected to accelerate y/y in FY26 with a pivot towards short-lived assets (GPUs/ CPUs) to support immediate capacity constraints. MSFT is certainly not an out of consensus pick, but in an environment where there are existential questions around the relevance of software, we think it makes sense to stick to a name like MSFT with one of the strongest AI stories."

Fitzsimmons puts an Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating on Microsoft, along with a $600 price target that suggests a one-year upside potential of 48% for the stock. (To watch Fitzsimmons' track record, click here)

There are 36 recent analyst reviews on file for Microsoft, and the 32 to 4 split, favoring Buy over Hold, gives the stock its Strong Buy consensus rating. The shares are priced at $404.37 and the average target price, of $593.38, indicates room for 47% growth in the coming year. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/12/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rise after Strong Jobs Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures climbed early Thursday following a stronger-than-expected January jobs report. Payrolls rose by 130,000, and the unemployment rate fell to 4.3%, easing concerns about the labor market. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.24%, 0.28%, and 0.26%, respectively, at 6:10 a.m. EST on February 12.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, all three indexes closed lower, with the Dow falling 0.13%, the S&P 500 dipping around 0.1%, and the Nasdaq down 0.16%.

After markets closed, Cisco Systems (CSCO) fell 7% despite beating Q2 expectations, due to weak guidance. Meanwhile, McDonald's (MCD) rose after surpassing earnings estimates.

Turning toward the economic calendar, data on initial jobless claims will be released.

On the earnings front, notable companies including Coinbase Global (COIN), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Roku (ROKU), and DraftKings (DKNG) will release earnings today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.17%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $64.36 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $5,061 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today amid positive corporate earnings.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on February 12. Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index dropped 0.86%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.05%, while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.80%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 0.02%, but the Topix increased 0.70% as part of the post-election rally.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Nvidia Stock Forecast Ahead of Earnings – Here's What to Expect
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is set to release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on February 25. With AI GPU demand staying robust, investors will look for confirmation that revenue and margins can support current valuations. The chip giant's results mark a pivotal moment for investors tracking its lead amid booming GPU demand and competitive pressures. With shares trading around $185–$190 recently, Wall Street anticipates robust results that could propel the stock higher or expose valuation risks.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia dominates the AI infrastructure market with an estimated 80% GPU share through its H100/H200 AI chips and emerging Blackwell platforms. At the same time, Nvidia faces an ongoing battle over China market recovery, Rubin GPU timelines, and hyperscaler spending. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged nearly 45%.

Expectations from Nvidia

According to Wall Street estimates, Nvidia is expected to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, up nearly 71% from the year-ago figure of $0.89. Moreover, sales are forecast to jump 67% year-over-year to $65.56 billion.

For full-year fiscal 2026, sales are projected at $213.3 billion and adjusted earnings at $4.69 per share. Data center sales have been a major revenue driver for Nvidia through the first nine months of the fiscal year, totaling $131.4 billion.

Analysts' Views Ahead of Results

Heading into the results, a majority of analysts have reiterated their Buy ratings on NVDA stock. UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri lifted his price target from $235 to $245, implying 28.9% upside. He noted that the supply chain remains strong and the setup into the next earnings period is favorable, particularly with the GTC developer conference coming up in mid-March.

Arcuri also believes Nvidia will exclude China sales from its guidance since market reopening is still uncertain amid rising domestic chip adoption. Profitability questions persist as peers build AI processors. UBS sees little near-term risk to Nvidia's roughly 75% gross margin and expects management to address competitive concerns during the earnings call and GTC.

Similarly, Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider maintained a $250 price target ahead of the results, suggesting 31.5% upside. He expects Nvidia's Q4 sales to hit $67.3 billion, implying a $2 billion revenue surprise above the consensus, and a bottom-line beat.

Schneider highlighted drivers that could push Nvidia's stock higher after the results, including stronger-than-expected hyperscalers' capex, positive comments on FY27 data-center sales forecast, a ramp in GPU demand from LLM companies, the competitive advantages of Nvidia's CUDA platform, China demand trends, and progress on Rubin production.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Nvidia stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 37 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $260.38 implies 37% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AppLovin Stock (APP) Falls Despite Beating Q4 Expectations, Wiliam Blair Stays Bullish
data.description:
AppLovin (APP) stock fell about 4% in Thursday's pre-market trading despite the ad-tech platform reporting better-than-expected results for the fourth quarter of 2025. The company also issued upbeat guidance for the first quarter of 2026. Heading into Q4 earnings, some analysts were concerned about APP stock's elevated valuation and growing competition in the digital ads market.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As of Wednesday's closing, APP stock was down 32.2% year-to-date, though it has risen about 22% over the past year.

Key Insights from AppLovin's Q4 Earnings

AppLovin reported a 66% growth in its Q4 2025 revenue to $1.66 billion, exceeding analysts' consensus estimate of $1.61 billion. The company attributed the top-line strength to continued technology advancements in its core mobile gaming business, seasonal strength, and the favorable impact of its e-commerce initiative.

Meanwhile, APP's adjusted earnings per share (EPS) jumped more than 87% year-over-year to $3.24 and crushed Wall Street's estimate of $2.95. The robust bottom line reflected strong revenue growth and solid margin expansion.

Looking ahead, AppLovin expects Q1 2026 revenue in the range of $1.745 billion to $1.775 billion and adjusted EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) in the range of $1.465 billion to $1.495 billion. The company also expects continued strength in its margins. While the Q1 2026 revenue guidance exceeded expectations, it indicates a slowdown (52% growth at midpoint) compared to the fourth quarter of 2025.

When questioned about growing competition in the digital ads space, CEO Adam Foroughi expressed confidence in AppLovin's expertise in the niche market.

William Blair Analyst Reacts Positively

Following the Q4 print, William Blair analyst Ralph Schackart reiterated a Buy rating on APP stock, saying that the company delivered the "strongest operating performance to date" despite AI (artificial intelligence) concerns.

He noted that management addressed bears' concerns during the earnings call to help mitigate the disconnect between current market sentiment and the company's operating performance. Notably, management highlighted that if AI was disrupting the business, it would be tough for the company to grow its revenue by 66% and achieve 84% adjusted EBITDA margin.

Addressing concerns about growing competition, Foroughi explained that AI tools like Google's (GOOGL) Project Genie can significantly reduce content-creation costs and lead to a surge in new games. That said, as more games enter the market, finding the best ones could become tough — and that's where advertisers will turn to AppLovin's platform.

Is APP a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on AppLovin stock based on 15 unanimous Buys. The average APP stock price target of $745.15 indicates 63.1% upside potential from current levels.

These ratings/price targets could change as more analysts react to AppLovin's Q4 results and guidance.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Rises as Chinese Watchdog Moves to Tame Cutthroat Price War
data.description:
American electric vehicle maker Tesla's (TSLA) shares rose fractionally on Thursday. The move came as Chinese authorities released new price-compliance guidance for automakers, including a ban on selling vehicles below production cost.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The new guide from the State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR) comes as Tesla in January offered zero-interest loans of up to five years to customers in China, igniting a new bout of competition over loan terms among automakers in the world's largest car market.

The guide also follows reports of weaker consumer demand for vehicles in January. The new rules apply to both carmakers, dealers, and parts suppliers.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
